entries.title: Resumable public API PriMock57 runner
entries.why: The site should not stop at one-note receipts. It needs a concrete path from the public Vercel lab to current aggregate PriMock57 evidence without committing raw generated notes.
entries.proof: Added scripts/run_public_api_benchmark.ts with ignored progress caching, fail-closed incomplete-case handling, configuration mismatch guards, aggregate-only pending output, and tests. A production slice generated and judged PM57-d1c01 through https://scribe-bench.vercel.app; PM57-d1c02 and PM57-d1c03 generated but judge calls first timed out, then hit OpenRouter's free-model daily cap on retry. The errored cases stayed excluded instead of being counted clean.
entries.next: Add OpenRouter credits or configure a faster second judge, then resume the cached run toward at least 30 completed PriMock57 cases before publishing any ranked current row.

entries.title: Receipt-first homepage framing
entries.why: The public site needed to answer the first-minute question more directly: what does a visitor do here, who is it for, and what do they leave with?
entries.proof: Reworked the hero around the after-the-demo clinical AI use case, added a first-screen ScribeBench receipt preview for SYN-003, clarified that visitors leave with a quotable QA receipt, and aligned the NapierMD footer link to the live clinical-AI work section.
entries.next: Keep moving from receipt-shaped one-note triage toward full powered PriMock57 rows for current models and real scribe workflows.

entries.title: Live PriMock57 smoke passed
entries.why: The public site needed proof that the current-model path is more than static copy: a visitor should be able to generate and judge a realistic PriMock57 encounter through production.
entries.proof: Production Vercel generated PM57-d1c01 with nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra-550b-a55b:free and judged the note through the public /api/judge endpoint with the same current OpenRouter free model. The smoke result returned normalized=100, inputFidelity=5, 0 dangerous fabrications, 0 leaks, and 3 standard/non-dangerous assumptions.
entries.next: Run the full PriMock57 set with repeats and publish only aggregate scores once the row has enough cases to support a system-level claim.

entries.title: Harder judge JSON repair
entries.why: The first PriMock57 current-row probe generated a note successfully but the OpenRouter judge returned malformed JSON, blocking a current powered evidence run.
entries.proof: Added JSON-object mode for OpenRouter judging, a third API repair attempt with prior-output context, parser repair for raw control characters inside strings plus trailing commas, and a plain-text compact fallback when models keep mangling JSON. A follow-up PM57 reference-note probe showed nvidia/nemotron-3-ultra-550b-a55b:free returns a parseable judge result while the previous Super judge path still failed on the generated PM57 note.
entries.next: Use the Ultra judge and compact fallback as the public OpenRouter default path, then rerun the PriMock57 current-row probe and graduate it into a full scores-only row once judge responses are stable.

entries.title: Current-model challenge planner
entries.why: The site now gives visitors a concrete answer to the stale-leaderboard objection: pick the kind of current row you can run, name the system, and copy the exact evidence plan.
entries.proof: Added the Current-model challenge section with target switching, system-label input, live proof-plan rendering, and clipboard/fallback copy behavior for frontier, open/free, real-workflow, and second-judge runs.
entries.next: Use the planner to turn public interest into actual powered PriMock57 rows for current models and real scribe workflows.

entries.title: Public claim checker
entries.why: A visitor can paste a vague AI-scribe claim, choose what kind of claim it is, and leave with a concrete evidence requirement plus a copyable public ask.
entries.proof: Added the Claim checker section to the public site with live claim-type switching and clipboard/fallback copy behavior for one-note, hallucination-free, system-comparison, and current-ranking claims.
entries.next: Use the claim checker to route public discussion into either the Lab evidence packet or a powered PriMock57 submission.

entries.title: Fresh OpenRouter smoke row
entries.why: The public Vercel site generated and judged all three bundled synthetic cases with current OpenRouter free models, so the board now has fresh evidence instead of only launch-era baselines.
entries.proof: Added openrouter-nemotron-3-ultra-live-smoke to the visible smoke table: n=3, nErrored=0, repeats=1, 0/3 dangerous fabrications flagged. The production judge JSON-repair path was exercised on SYN-003.
entries.next: Run the same generator over PriMock57 with declared repeats before treating it as a system-level claim.

entries.title: Lab evidence packet
entries.why: A visitor can now leave the Lab with a compact packet that names the scope, case, generator, judge, finding, and next proof step.
entries.proof: Public alias serves the Evidence packet UI and copy action; production logs were clean after deploy.
entries.next: Use packets as the lightweight artifact for public discussion, then graduate useful claims into PriMock57 rows.

entries.title: One-click current-model smoke check
entries.why: The homepage can run the seeded fall case through the configured OpenRouter free-model path instead of only describing the benchmark.
entries.proof: OpenRouter model discovery is configured on production and returns current free models; the live site exposes the smoke-check CTA.
entries.next: Promote promising smoke checks to powered PriMock57 runs before making any model-ranking claim.

entries.title: Contribution command builder
entries.why: The Run section now turns a visitor's dataset, generator, judge, repeats, and system label into the JSON shape and command needed for a public row.
entries.proof: The builder generates smoke and powered commands, candidate-note JSON templates, and a PR checklist from the selected run type.
entries.next: Use the builder to add current frontier, open/free, real-workflow, and judge-robustness rows.
